programs.title: Dreams beyond the acrobatic stage
programs.desc: Many young acrobats from across China have gathered in Wuqiao, Cangzhou in north China's Hebei Province, with one shared goal: to dazzle audiences with their talents and stunt skills. They usually starts to train at an early age, shouldering the hopes and support of their families and teachers to become successful performers. We followed three of these young performers to show you how incredibly talented they are.

programs.title: Echoes of Green
programs.desc: Echoes of Green takes viewers deep into Anji’s bamboo forests and mountain villages, where a new generation of Chinese youth is redefining how to live with nature. Through stories of outdoor sports, forest cafés, tea rituals, and family farms, the series shows how ecological ideals have become everyday choices and creative lifestyles. It is not just about landscapes, but about people — young dreamers proving that prosperity and nature can grow side by side, and that culture gives life to sustainability.

programs.title: Quyang Stone Carving
programs.desc: Quyang is known as a hometown of Chinese carving. Quyang's stone carving works are popular around different parts of China, thus also exported to over 100 countries and regions worldwide. The style of Quyang stone carving both carries the mysterious and rough spirit of the Wei Dynasty and the natural and graceful forms of the Tang and Song dynasties. It reflects the life experiences and live philosophies of local artisans. Each statue's showcases unique oriental aesthetics.

programs.title: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade: A Traditional Chinese Silk Craft
programs.desc: Nanjing Yunjin Brocade, a traditional Chinese silk handicraft with a history spanning over 1,600 years, derives its name from its radiant and resplendent hues, which resemble clouds in the sky. Renowned for its exquisite materials, meticulous weaving techniques, and vibrant, splendid patterns, Yunjin Brocade is hailed as the \"king of brocades.\" It represents the pinnacle of Chinese silk weaving techniques, encapsulates the essence of Chinese silk craftsmanship, and stands as a brilliant crystallization of China’s silk culture. The core technique of Yunjin Brocade weaving—known as \"weaving through the warp and cutting the weft\"—is executed manually by two artisans working above and below complex, large-scale looms. Using materials such as silk floss, gold thread, and peacock feather threads, they create luxurious fabrics like imperial dragon robes. To this day, Yunjin Brocade continues to be used in crafting high-end clothing and souvenirs. Creating Yunjin Brocade involves over 120 intricate and sophisticated processes before the final masterpiece takes shape. Having traversed over a millennium, its majestic and luxurious aura remains unchanged. Just like the Qinhuai River, which has flowed through Nanjing for over 3,000 years—clear and steadfast—its gentle currents cut through the annals of history as it journeys toward an even more distant future.

programs.title: CULTURAL INHERITORS Ancient Ru Kiln embraces its new era
programs.desc: The Vibe production team's meticulously crafted documentary series \"Cultural Inheritors\". The eight episodes span across various regions of China, focusing on the inheritors and artists of intangible cultural heritage. They explore and innovate with artisanal dedication, showcasing the extraordinary craftsmanship of fields such as the Ru Kiln porcelain, colored stone inlay, Pu'er tea, and Lingnan woodwork. Let’s embark on a cultural journey together, savoring the beauty of tradition and embracing the spirit of intangible cultural heritage.

programs.title: A Vibe of the Village: The Beat of Xiangxi Drums
programs.desc: Experience the vibrant power of Xiangxi Drum Dance. This program delves into the heart of Tujia and Miao cultures, showcasing the rhythmic beats and spirited performances that have echoed through West Hunan's mountains for centuries. Discover the stories, traditions, and raw energy of this captivating folk art.
